mnemonics.title: 7 Layers Top to Bottom
mnemonics.content: All People Seem To Need Data Processing - A(P)plication, (P)resentation, (S)ession, (T)ransport, (N)etwork, (D)ata Link, (P)hysical

mnemonics.title: 7 Layers Bottom to Top
mnemonics.content: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away - (P)hysical, (D)ata Link, (N)etwork, (T)ransport, (S)ession, (P)resentation, (A)plication

mnemonics.title: Alternative Bottom to Top
mnemonics.content: A Piano Stored In The NaveL Demonstrates Prosperity - A(plication), P(resentation), S(ession), I(n), T(he), N(etwork), L(ayer), D(emonstrates), P(rosperity)

mnemonics.title: PDU at Each Layer
mnemonics.content: Data Creates Network Trouble: Data (7-5), Creates (4-Segment), Network (3-Packet), Trouble (2-Frame), : (1-Bits)

acronyms.usage: The conceptual model developed by ISO for standardizing network communication
The organization that developed the OSI model in 1984
The data unit at each OSI layer: Data, Segment, Packet, Frame, Bits
Physical address at Data Link Layer, 48-bit hardware address
Logical addressing and routing protocol at Network Layer
Application Layer protocol for web browsing
Application Layer protocol for domain name resolution
Application Layer protocol for file transfer

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a building with 7 floors. Ground floor is Physical (1), top floor is Application (7). Data flows down from top (encapsulation) and up from bottom (de-encapsulation).

Think of nested boxes: Application data goes into a box (Transport header), that box goes into another (Network header), another (Data Link header), finally bits travel on wire. Reverse at receiving end - unwrap each box.
Hub = Layer 1 (repeats bits), Switch = Layer 2 (reads frames with MAC), Router = Layer 3 (reads packets with IP). Think: More floors = smarter device.
Physical address (MAC) = house number on a street (local), Logical address (IP) = street address (routing across city). Port = apartment number (specific app in house).

analogies.analogy: Like a postal system: Application (writing letter), Presentation (putting in envelope/language), Session (address verification), Transport (sorting/post office), Network (routing between cities), Data Link (local delivery van), Physical (roads/trucks).
Like an onion - each layer adds a protective wrapper. To send: add coat, add box, add pallet. To receive: remove pallet, remove box, remove coat to get original item.
TCP is like registered mail with delivery confirmation. UDP is like sending a postcard - faster but no guarantee it arrives or in what order.
Data Link is like neighbors talking on the same street (MAC addresses). Network is like sending mail to different cities (IP addresses for routing).
